payload.text: Allow users to select words by letter. Eg there should be an easy way to see all words starting with "S" and so on.

**Developing New Filters**

I'm currently focused on how to best implement the letter-based filtering for Swar-Takshari. The existing category filter and search bar provide some functionality, but a dedicated letter selection system would significantly enhance usability. I'm exploring different UI options to make this addition intuitive and efficient for the user.


**Designing Letter Filtering**

I've made considerable progress on integrating the letter-based filtering. The `FilterState` is updated to include the `startingLetter` property. I'm also working on the filtering logic within `App.tsx` and have derived the available letters dynamically from `BOLLYWOOD_WORDS`. The plan now is to add the UI component, a horizontal, scrollable alphabet picker, right below the category filters. I want the design to blend seamlessly with both themes.


**Improving UI Flow**

My recent focus has been on refining the alphabet picker's placement and functionality. I've decided to integrate it as a second row of filter buttons right after the category filter, keeping the design consistent. Implementing a "Reset/All" option is a priority. I'm also ensuring the filtering logic, including the unique starting letter derivation, works seamlessly with the existing faceted search behavior, as you suggested.


**Confirming Filter Logic**

I'm now fully committed to the filtering logic. I've defined `startingLetter` as a `string | 'All'` and derived `availableLetters` dynamically. The filter itself now correctly applies the `startsWith` method based on the selected letter. Confirmed it works as intended! Now I just need to put it together.

entries.description: Update FilterState to include startingLetter for filtering words by alphabet.
Add alphabet picker and update filtering logic to support starting letter selection.

payload.text: sort the words in alphabetical order

**Considering Alphabetical Order**

I'm currently focused on the alphabetical sorting feature for the "Swar-Takshari" app. Examining the codebase, I've identified `constants.ts` holding the `BOLLYWOOD_WORDS` array. `App.tsx` filters this array based on search, category, and starting letter. Now, I need to incorporate the alphabetical sort within this existing filtering logic.


**Adding Alphabetical Sort**

I'm now incorporating the alphabetical sort into the `filteredWords` memo within `App.tsx`. The default and filtered lists will both have this sort. I plan to append `.sort((a, b) => a.word.localeCompare(b.word))` to the existing filtering chain within the `useMemo` hook. This approach ensures consistent alphabetical ordering across all displayed word lists. This ensures the alphabetical scroller in `App.tsx` corresponds to the sort.


**Prioritizing Word Sorting**

I've shifted my focus to the alphabetical word sorting within `App.tsx`. I realized the existing alphabetical sorting of available letters is complete. Now, I'm integrating `.sort((a, b) => a.word.localeCompare(b.word))` into `filteredWords` to sort the word list itself. This ensures the alphabetical scroller aligns perfectly with the displayed words.


**Verifying Filtering Logic**

Okay, I've implemented the alphabetical sort within the `useMemo` hook, right after the filtering logic. Now, I'm verifying the entire `filteredWords` calculation within `App.tsx`. I need to ensure the sorting doesn't interfere with the other filters, such as search, category, and starting letter. A quick review of the code seems good.


**Analyzing App Component**

I'm now diving deeper into the `App.tsx` component. I want to understand how different states are affecting the component's render lifecycle. I'm focusing on potential performance bottlenecks. I need to ensure the alphabetical sorting and all other filters are executed efficiently, especially with a large dataset of words. I'll probably start using the React Dev Tools to understand the render times.

entries.description: Sort the filtered words in alphabetical order by the Hindi word property.
